testimonials.label: Testimonials
testimonials.title: Loved by
testimonials.subtitle: See what developers around the world are saying about SQL Pro.

quotes.developer: SQL Pro has completely transformed how I work with SQLite databases. The Monaco editor with autocomplete is a game changer.
quotes.datascientist: Finally, a database tool that just works. The visual diff preview saves me hours every week when reviewing data changes.
quotes.indie: As a solo developer, I need tools that are fast and reliable. SQL Pro is exactly that - simple, powerful, and beautiful.

opensource.label: Open Source
opensource.title: Built in the Open,
opensource.description: SQL Pro is fully open source under the MIT license. Inspect the code, contribute features, or fork it for your own needs.

faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about SQL Pro.

free.question: Is SQL Pro free to use?
free.answer: Yes! SQL Pro is open source and free to use under the MIT license. You can download and use it for personal and commercial projects at no cost.

platforms.question: Which platforms are supported?
platforms.answer: SQL Pro runs natively on macOS (Intel and Apple Silicon), Windows (x64), and Linux (x64). We provide Universal binaries for Mac and AppImage/deb packages for Linux.

sqlite.question: What SQLite features are supported?
sqlite.answer: SQL Pro supports all standard SQLite features including views, triggers, indexes, foreign keys, virtual tables, and full-text search. We also support WAL mode and concurrent connections.

encryption.question: How does database encryption work?
encryption.answer: SQL Pro supports SQLCipher encrypted databases. Your encryption keys are stored securely in your system's keychain (macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager, or Linux Secret Service).

updates.question: How do I get updates?
updates.answer: SQL Pro includes automatic update checks. When a new version is available, you'll be notified and can update with a single click. You can also download updates manually from GitHub Releases.

support.question: Where can I get help?
support.answer: You can get help through our GitHub Discussions for community support, GitHub Issues for bug reports, or our documentation for guides and tutorials.
